SENATE
Pasay City
JOURNAL
Senate Sitting as An Impeachment Court On the Impeachment Trial of Vice-President Sara Zimmerman Duterte impeachment Trial No. 5 Monday, 13 July 2026
PREPARED BY THE LEGISLATIVE JOURNAL SERVICE
SENATE OF THE PHILIPPINES
IMPEACHMENT TRIAL NO. 5
Monday, July 13, 2026
CALL TO ORDER

At 2:00 p.m., Monday, July 13, 2026, the Senate President, Hon. Win Gatchalian, with the Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court, called the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte to order.
PRAYER

blocks.speaker: Sen. Joseph Victor “JV” G
blocks.text: Ejercito led the prayer, to wit:

Ama naming makapangyarihan, lumalapit kami sa lyo na may pagpapakumbaba. Batid namin na ang karunungan ng tao ay may hangganan, ngunit ang lyong katotohanan at katarungan ay nananatili magpakailanman Sa pagtupad namin ng aming tungkulin bilang mga Senator-Judges, hiniihiling namin ang lyong gabay sa lahat ng aming gagawin. Pagkalooban Mo nawa kami ng tapat na puso at malinaw na pag-iisip sa buong panahon ng paglilitis na ito. Panginoon, nakikiusap po kaming akayin Mo kami sa landas na makatarungan na may patas na pagtingin sa karapatan ng bawat isa. Dalhin Ninyo kami sa liwanag, sapagkat nakapiring ang aming mga mata. Hindi man po namin hiningi ngunit ipinagkatiwala sa aming Konstitusyon ang timbangan ng katarungan. Kumiling nawa ito sa karapat-dapat na pagkalooban nito.

Nawa ang Inyong Salita ang maging gabay sa aming paglilingkod at pagdala sa amin sa katotohanan. Kagaya po ng winika sa Leviticus 19:15: “Do not pervert justice, do not show partiality, but judge your neighbor fairly.” Lagi po naming tatandaan ang tagubilin Ninyong ito.

Ama, anuman ang maging bunga ng paglilitis na ito, hindi nawa ito makita bilang tagumpay ng isa at pagkatalo ng iba, kundi para sa ikabubuti ng sambayanang Pilipino. Lahat po ng aming gagawin ay para sa ikalulugod Mo at hindi para sa kapakinabangan ng sinumang tao, mga partido, o kanilang mga pansariling interes, kundi ito’y para sa pagpapatibay ng aming mga institusyon, ng pamahalaan, at sa pagpapanatili ng tiwala ng mamamayan sa demokrasya ng aming bayan.
Buong puso po kaming nananalangin na sa lahat ng aming gagawin, lagi’t lagi namin Kayong pinupuri.
Amen.
ROLL CALL
Upon direction of the Senate President, the Clerk of Court, Atty. Renato N. Bantug Jr., called the roll, to which the following senator-judges responded: <r
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026
Marcos, I. R. Padilla, R. Sotto in, V. C. Tulfo, E. T. Tulfo, R. T. Villanueva, J. Villar, C. A. Villar, M. A. Zubiri, J. M. F.
Aquino, B. Cayetano, P. S. Ejercito, J. V. Escudero, F. G. Gatchalian, W. T. Go, C. L. T. Hontiveros, R. Lacson, P. M. Lapid, M. M.
With 18 senator-judges present, the Chair declared the presence of a quorum.
Senator-Judges Cayetano (A) and Legarda were also present.
Senator-Judges Estrada and Marcoleta were under detention and unable to attend.
Senator-Judges Dela Rosa and Pangilinan were absent.
PROCLAMATION OF THE SERGEANT-AT-ARMS

The Sergeant-at-Arms, PMGen Alfred S. Corpus, commanded all persons, on pain of penalty, to keep silent while the Senate is sitting for the trial of the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte.
APPROVAL OF THE JOURNAL

Upon motion of the Presiding Officer, Senator Francis “Chiz” G. Escudero, there being no objection, the Body dispensed with the reading of the Journal of the Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court on July 8, 2026 and considered the same as approved.
CALLING OF THE CASE
Upon direction of the Presiding Officer, the Clerk of Court called Case No. 004-2026, titled “In the Matter of the Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte. ”
APPEARANCES
The Presiding Officer directed the parties to enter their appearances.
For the prosecution. Prosecutor Lorenz R. Defensor entered the appearances previously entered on behalf of the prosecution.
For the Respondent, Atty. Sheila Sison entered the same appearances.
INQUIRY OF SENATOR-JUDGE TULFO (R)

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Tulfo (R) sought clarification from the counsel for the defendant, Atty
blocks.text: Mark Vinluan, regarding his earlier statement that Vice President Duterte’s response to questions about the alleged “Operation Romanov” was unconvential but justified, considering the threat on her and her family. He confirmed whether it was the same “Operation Romanov” mentioned by vlogger Princess Maui during the Vice President’s online press conference. Atty. Vinluan answered in the affirmative.
MONDAY, JULY 13,2026

Asked whether Princess Maui would be presented as a defense witness, Attorney Vinluan said the defense reserved the right to determine its witnesses depending on the evidence presented by the prosecution but declined to identify them at this stage.

Senator-Judge Tulfo (R) noted that he first heard the term “Operation Romanov” during the Maisug Leaders’ Forum on January 28, 2024 in Davao City, where it was mentioned by Mayor Sebastian Duterte, and asked whether it was related to the references made by Princess Maui and Atty. Vinluan.

In reply. Attorney Vinluan said he could not comment since he had no personal knowledge of Mayor Duterte’s statement. When asked what he meant when he said that the Vice President’s response as unconventional. Attorney Vinluan explained that the term was merely a description of the statements she made during the press conference and that the word should be understood in its ordinary meaning.
MANIFESTATION OF PROSECUTOR DEFENSOR

Before the examination of the prosecution’s second witness. Prosecutor Defensor informed the Court that they received a letter dated July 10, 2026, from Director Melvin A. Matibag of the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), requesting that his testimony be scheduled at the earliest opportunity due to his attendance in a regional summit on scam center compounds and transnational organized crime organized by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) and the Royal Thai Police. He requested that Director Matibag testify the following day immediately after the testimony of the prosecution’s second witness, Atty. Jeremy A. Lotoc.

The Presiding Officer sought clarification, noting that Atty. Zuleika Lopez, Chief of Staff of the Office of the Vice President (OVP) had previously been scheduled to testify the following day. Prosecutor Defensor confirmed the existing schedule and explained that, after Director Matibag’s testimony, the proceedings would continue with the previously announced order of witnesses, including Attorney Lopez and Capt. Belinda Bello, head of the Legislative Security Bureau of the House of Representatives (HOR).

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) stated that he would not object to the request if there were no objections from the Members but expressed concern over accommodating the request, cautioning that allowing a bureau head to dictate the Court’s schedule could set a bad precedent and affect the orderly presentation of evidence. He emphasized that the senator-judges had likewise set aside official responsibilities to attend the impeachment trial.

In response. Prosecutor Defensor explained that it was only requesting to advance Director Matibag’s testimony by one day without altering the sequence of the remaining witnesses, while stating that the prosecution would abide by the Court’s ruling.

The Presiding Officer recalled the prosecution’s previous statements that Director Matibag would not testify during the current week and that only Attorney Lotoc, Attorney Lopez, and Captain Bello will be presented as this week’s witnesses. He pointed out that Director Matibag had not been included in the witness list and that no subpoena had been issued requiring his appearance during the week, correcting the statement that the testimony is merely being advanced by one day.

Prosecutor Defensor clarified that, while the prosecution had earlier identified its witnesses for the week, it also informed the Court that it would later specify the date of Director Matibag’s testimony after requesting the issuance of a subpoena. He said the prosecution had intended to present Director Matibag on Wednesday, after the testimony of Captain Bello. The Presiding Officer acknowledged the explanation but noted that, in light of the one-witness-per-day rule.
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026
no subpoena had been issued to Director Matibag for the week. Upon the Presiding Officer’s request for confirmation, the Clerk of Court confirmed that no subpoena had been issued.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) clarified that his earlier remarks were not directed at the prosecution but recalled that the change in schedule affects the preparation of the senator-judges. After seeking clarification on the applicable notice rules from the Presiding Officer, he reiterated the importance of adhering to the Court’s procedures to ensure the orderly and efficient conduct of the impeachment trial.
OBJECTION OF ATTORNEY VINLUAN

Attorney Vinluan opposed the prosecution’s request to advance the testimony of Director Matibag, arguing that it would violate the Court’s previous directive on the order of witnesses, the five-day notice requirement, and the principles of fair play and procedural due process. He maintained that the defense had prepared its case based on the previously announced witness schedule and that a last-minute change would prejudice its ability to conduct an effective cross- examination. Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the five-day notice period is for witnesses not mentioned in the pre-trial order.

Attorney Vinluan also requested that the prosecution provide the defense with a copy of Director Matibag’s invitation to the international conference and disclose whether a corresponding travel authority had been issued. He explained that the defense had received information that a similar FBI regional summit had already been held in Bangkok the previous month and wished to verify whether Director Matibag’s attendance justified the requested modification of the witness schedule.
REMARKS OF SENATE PRESIDENT GATCHALIAN

Senate President Gatchalian expressed support for the manifestation of Senator-Judge Cayetano (A), emphasizing that the senator-judges, like the prosecution and defense, prepare in advance for the scheduled presentation of witnesses. He noted that many senator-judges devoted the weekend to studying the expected testimonies and preparing their questions based on the previously announced witness schedule.

Senate President Gatchalian reiterated that the senator-judges devoted considerable time to reviewing the voluminous documentary evidence and preparing questions for the scheduled witnesses. He cautioned that the last-minute inclusion of additional witnesses would deprive the senator-judges of sufficient time to study the evidence and prepare their inquiries, thereby affecting the orderly and fair conduct of the proceedings.

Reading the transcript verbatim. Presiding Officer Escudero confirmed the prosecution’s earlier manifestation that while Director Melvin Matibag had not been scheduled to testify during the week, it would determine by today whether to present him on Wednesday, depending on the progress of the trial.
ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Deferring action on the prosecution’s request, the Presiding Officer directed the prosecution to submit Director Matibag’s letter, furnishing copies to the respondent’s counsel. Upon the defense’s manifestation, he further ordered the prosecution to submit any available travel authority and supporting documents, including the official invitation from the foreign organizers, if already issued. He clarified that the documents would be considered solely for the Court’s evaluation of the request and directed that the defense be furnished copies and given the opportunity to comment before the Court rules on the matter.
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026
PRESENTATION OF THE PROSECUTION’S SECOND WITNESS

The Presiding Officer directed the prosecution to proceed with the presentation of its second witness and recognized Atty. Virgil Ligutan, counsel for the prosecution, to conduct the examination. Attorney Ligutan called on Atty. Jeremy C. Lotoc of the NBI to the witness stand, after which the Clerk of Court administered his oath.
OFFER OF COUNSEL FOR THE PROSECUTION

Attorney Ligutan offered the testimony of Attorney Lotoc to establish his position as Chief of the NBI Cybercrime Division in 2024; his participation in the bureau’s investigation into the respondent’s alleged threats during the November 23, 2024 online media briefing; the NBI’s findings that the respondent’s statements constituted serious and credible threats, which led to the filing of complaints for three counts of grave threats and one count of inciting to sedition; and the authenticity of the NBI affidavit of investigation, its annexes, related video recordings, and other matters material to the impeaehment proceedings.
The respondent’s counsel interposed no objection to the offer of testimony, subject to cross- examination.
DIRECT EXAMINATION OF THE WITNESS
Attorney Ligutan proceeded to make the following direct examination of the witness:

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Daghang salamat, Mr. Presiding Officer. Honorable Judges.
Attorney Lotoc, maupay na kulop ha imo.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Maupay man. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Waray-Waray po. We are both Waray-Waray, at the same time Cebuano.
Atty. Lotoc, puwede bang mas malapit pa sa microphone para marinig ng sambayanan lahat ng isasagot mo sa mga tanong ko.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.
Now, Mr. Lotoc, kindly state your name—edad, and other personal circumstances.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I am Atty. Jeremy C. Lotoc, 50 years of age, and currently the Regional Director of the NBI Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Sinabi mo ikaw ay isang abogado, Atty. Lotoc, ‘di ba?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Opo.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, iyong tanong: kailan ka naging miyembro ng ating Philippine Bar?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I was admitted to the Philippine Bar and took my Lawyer’s Oath in 2013, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: 2013. Now, Mr. Lotoc, ano ba iyong current position mo, occupation mo, kung mayroon manl

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: The Regional Director IV of the NBI Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Ikaw ay isang NBI Regional Director IV for BARMM. Now, iyong tanong is: how long have you been with the NBI?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I’ve been in the NBI for the past 12 years. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: 12 years, ano? Ang tanong is—based on my counting that would be since 2014, tama po bal
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Opo, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, kailan ka ba na-designate as the NBI Regional Director ng BARMM?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I was designated in January this year as Regional Director of the NBI, BARMM, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: January 2026. Now, prior to your designation as the NBI Regional Director for BARMM, ano ba iyong previous position mo within NBI?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I was the Regional Director for Region 4B or MIMAROPA, Sir. I think July 2025.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: July 2025, you became—okay. Now, prior to your designation as NBI Regional Director for MIMAROPA, what was your previous position, kung mayroon man?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I was the Chief of the NBI Cybercrime Division, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Chief ng NBI Cybercrime Division. Now, kailan ka na-designate as Chief ng NBI Cybercrime Division?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I was designated in February 2023, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: 2023, ‘no? Now, sinabi mo ikaw ay naging Chief ng NBI Cybercrime Division. Now, iyong tanong, Atty. Lotoc is: what were your primary duties and functions bilang hepe ng NBI Cybercrime Division?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Primary duties, among others: to plan, direct, supervise the investigation of cybercrime cases pursuant to Republic Act No. 10175 and other relevant laws; and part of my duties and responsibilities is to investigate high-profile cases pursuant to the Republie Act No. 10867, specifically crimes committed by, through, and with the use of information and communication technology. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, Mr. Witness, of course, you are aware na ikaw ay tumitestigo sa impeach­ ment trial ng respondent. Now, iyong tanong is: kilala mo ba iyong respondent sa kasong ito?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. Iyong tanong now is: why do you know her?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: She is the incumbent Vice President of the Philippines, and she is the subject of the investigation by the NBI Cybercrime Division, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: So, nasabi mo she is the subject of an investigation ng NBI Cybercrime Division. Now, iyong tanong, Atty. Lotoc, is: kailan ba? Kailan nagsimula iyong investigation ng NBI
Cybercrime Division on the Respondent?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: November 23, 2024, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: November 23, 2024. Iyong tanong now, Atty. Lotoc, is: why? Why did the Cybercrime Division commence an investigation on the Vice President?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: We were directed by the then-Director-Judge Jaime B. Santiago to conduct an investigation with regard to a certain video involving an online press conference held on November 23, 2024, where the Vice President uttered that she had already contracted a person to kill the president, Liza Marcos, and former Speaker of the House, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, iyong tanong, Atty. Lotoc, is: sa pagkakaalam mo—kung alam mo—bd’t iyong NBI iyong inatasang gumawa ng imbestigasyon na ito?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, under the law, specifically Republic Act No. 10867, the NBI has primary jurisdiction to undertake investigations oh cases among others, cases involving threats to security,
an assault against the persons of the highest officials. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: So, threats or assaults on or against highest officials, ang sabi mo, di ba? Now, iyong tanong, Atty. Lotoc, is: sino ba iyong mga highest officials na kung may threat or may assault sa kanila, NBI has to do an investigation? Sino iyong mga officials na ito?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor. They are the President, the Vice President, the Senate President, the Speaker of the House, and the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court, Sir.
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: All right. Now, so you mentioned Presidente, Vice President, at saka iyong iba pa. Now, you mentioned, Atty. Lotoc, that the NBI conducted an investigation on the respondent’s
statements during an online media briefing on November 23, 2024.
Iyong tanong now, Atty. Lotoc, is: what exactly did the respondent say on that day that resulted in the investigation?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: On that online press conference held on November 23, 2024, the Vice President uttered the following: “Huwag kang mag-alala, ma’am, sa security ko sapagkat may kinausap na akong tao. Ang sabi ko sa kaniya, pag pinatay ako, patayin mo si BBM, si Liza Marcos, si Martin Romualdez—no joke, no joke. Nagbilin na ako, ma ’am. Ang sabi ko sa kanya, pag namatay ako, huwag kang tumigil hangga’t hindi mo sila mapapatay. And he said yes.”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, Atty. Lotoc, iyon iyong statement. Now, sino ba iyong mga miyembro, miyembro ng investigation team na gating sa division mo, iyong Cybercrime Division?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: We have Atty. Van Angluben, the then Executive Officer for Operations ng Cybercrime Division; Atty. Vanessa Asuncion, the Executive Officer for Administration back then; John Mark Calilung, the agent of case; and myself, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: You mentioned agent John Mark Calilung.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: The same person who testified before you?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, Mr. Witness, how did your division commence the investigation?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I instructed the team, specifically agent Calilung, that with this particular case, we have to follow the five phases or stages of Cybercrime Investigation, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: So you instructed John Mark Calilung to follow four stages of the investigation?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: The team and John Mark Calilung, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, you mentioned Atty. Lotoc, iyong four stages ng Cybercrime Division for the information ng ating honorable judges at saka iyong publiko. This is the question now: ano ba iyong four stages ng investigation na ito?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, for that particular case, first, the identification and presentation of the digital evidence; the collection and hashing of that same digital evidence - that’s the second phase; the analysis and attribution phase, the third one; and then the legal evaluation and case filing as the final phase. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr, Ligutan
blocks.text: So, these are the four stages. Now, if you know, what was agent Calilung’s response and, if any, sa naging instruction mo sa kaniya na sundin iyong four stages ng Cybercrime Investigation?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, he said yes, and then he proceeded to identify; he proceeded to, yeah, identify and preserve.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Identify—I’m sorry, continue.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Identify and preserve the digital evidence in question. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, you said that you instructed agent Calilung to identify and preserve «u-mention mo, “digital evidence.”
Ang tanong now is, what is this evidence that you are referring to?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: It refers to a certain video. Your Honor, involving an online press conference held on November 23, 2024, which was streamed through the Facebook page of one, Atty. Herminio Lopez Roque Jr. or Harry Roque, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: So, it was the Facebook page of Harry Roque who has livestreamed the video?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. Now, Mr. Witness, after Agent Calilung identified and preserved iyong sinasabi mong video, which you said is the first stage of the investigation, am iyong nangyari after that, kung meron man, at saka kung alam mo?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor. The agent on case proceeded to collect that digital evidence and generated a hash value of that digital evidence, and at the same time, stored that in a data carrier. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr, Ligutan
blocks.text: Alam mo, Attorney Lotoc, these are the first time na narinig natin iyong term na “hash value.” I think it is just proper na iyong next question ko, para malaman ng publiko. Attorney Lotoc, ano ba iyong “hash value” na iyan?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, under the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 10175, or the Reorganization and Modernization Act of NBI, hash value refers to mathematical algorithm applied to the information, like the file, the physical disk, or the logical disk—in this case, the digital evidence—thereby creating a digital fingerprint or DNA for that information or digital evidence. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: So, to create a fingerprint for the video. Is that what you are saying?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor. In this case, the video.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: All right. Now, Mr. Witness, after the identification, preservation, collection, and hashing—and so far these are the two stages—what happened next, if any, in relation to your investigation on the Vice President’s statement?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: We proceeded to the analysis and attribution phase.

blocks.speaker: Mr, Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, ano iyong nangyari, kung meron man, at saka kung alam mo, in this analysis and attribution phase?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: In the analysis, we established the intent, the motive, the capability of the Vice President to carry out the threat she mentioned, as well as we proceeded to identify the individuals present in that press conference on November 23, 2024, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. So, sinabi mo, you proceeded to identify the different individuals in that meeting. Siguro iyong publiko, they are now interested. So, what happened? What happened pagkatapos ninyong mag-proceed sa pag-identify, sino ba iyong nasa video na iyan?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: First, we have identified media personalities from Philippine Daily Inquirer, from the Manila Times, from the Reuters, SMNI, et cetera. And then we have also identified the Vice President herself, who, in her presence, openly and publicly, uttered that she had already contracted a person to kill the President, Liza Marcos, and the former Speaker of the House, and instructed the person to be relentless in killing the three, and it was streamed through the Facebook page, again, of Harry Roque, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: You said to be relentless in the killing. Okay.
Now, after finding out that indeed the Vice President uttered that, “to be relentless in the killing,” what happened next, if any, in relation to the investigation you conducted?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: We continued the open-source investigation to determine if there were utterances made by the Vice President in the previous dates. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, ano ba iyong naging resulta, kung meron man, at saka kung alam mo, sa naging open-source investigation ng NBI into sinasabi mong mga previous statements ng Vice President?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: We have found a press conference dated October 18, 2024, streamed through the official Facebook page of the Office of the Vice President, and we were able to identify—for us, being the investigator—at least three material lines or utterances made by the Vice President, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. So, you said in that press conference, sinabi mo, you found three utterances or statements ng Vice President.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Among others, Sir, yes.
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Among others. So, let’s focus on these three. Ano ba iyong three statements na sinasabi mo?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, the first one, the Vice President uttered: “Gusto kong tanggalin iyong ulo niya ba. ” And then the second one, the Vice President uttered: “I realized toxic na itong relationship,” referring to the relationship between the Vice President and the President.
And then number three, “I imagine myself cutting his head.”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. Now, recap, sinabi mo, “Gusto kong tanggalin iyong ulo niya. ” And then sinabi mo rin sa third, “I imagine cutting his head.”
Based on your investigation. Attorney Lotoc, kaninong ulo ba iyong rme-refer ng Vice President na gusto niyang tanggalin?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: The President of the Republic of the Philippines, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Iyong ulo ng Presidente ng Republika ng Pilipinas.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Opo.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: What proof, if any, do you have that the Vice President said this?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: We have that video. Your Honor, dated October 18, 2024.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Now, kung maaalala mo, Mr. Witness, saang portion—or portion ng video na iyan maririnig iyong Vice President sinasabi iyong mga katagang “Gusto kong tanggalin iyong ulo niya... I imagine myself cutting his head?”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, if I may refer to my notes?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, I move that—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Witness may do so.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Thank you so much.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc

MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) observed that Article IV of the Articles of Impeachment alleged that Vice President Duterte committed culpable violation of the Constitution, high crimes, and betrayal of public trust by contracting for the assassination of the President, the First Lady, and the former Speaker of the HOR, as well as by making grave threats and actively inciting sedition. He noted that, from the prosecution’s standpoint, the alleged threats themselves, whether constituting high crimes, grave threats, or culpable violations of the Constitution were being treated as impeachable offenses.

He pointed out, however, that the Vice President did not explicitly state that she had contracted an assassin but merely stated that she had spoken to someone. Accordingly, he said that two matters required clarification: first, whether the alleged threat, by itself, constituted an impeachable offense; and second, whether there was sufficient proof that the Vice President had in fact contracted an actual assassin.
The Presiding Officer informed Attorney Ligutan that there was no need to respond to the manifestation of Senator-Judge Cayetano (A).

blocks.text: You can ask Atty. Ria Vanessa D. S. Asuncion again. Sir, kung anong nangyari riyan. Insofar as I am concerned, kumpleto ho iyong tatlo—1, 2, 3—ito ho iyong pang-apat.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Siguro, kailangan kong tanungin si XO kung ano ang nangyari riyan.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. So, let’s go to the affidavits that were executed after the interviews. Okay. Pahabol na ito lahat, tama? Kasi this was pursuant to the Feb. 5 certification, eh. So hinabol ninyo ito lahat, tama?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Hindi hinabol, kinomply ho namin iyong requirement ng DOJ.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: You want to use the term kinomply. Okay, sige. The interviews were in December—November to December 2024—but the affidavits were in February 10, 2025, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Again, Your Honor?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Iyong mga interview mismo, November to December 2024, kinonduct. Pew iyong mga affidavit attesting to those interviews, February of 2025 na /^-execute, tama?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Based sa document. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: At any rate, this was made pursuant to the Feb. 5 DOJ certification, correct?
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Can I see the document. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Okay. So in short, hinanapan kayo ng DOJ ng affidavits that’s why you attached additional ones to the revised Affidavit of Investigation. Is that what happened?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Again, the affidavit was not revised. May mga attachment lang ho na m-re quire yung Department of Justice. So, kinomply (comply) ho namin at saka sinubmit (submit) ho namin.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Yeah. Even your own director referred to it as revised, ‘di ba?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Objection, argumentative.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Let’s not make an issue about that.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Exactly.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: I’m just asking if these were attached to the second judicial Affidavit of Investigation. So, in other words, the agent who executed these affidavits were John Mark Calilung, Gierran, and Asuncion, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: And each agent executed their own affidavit, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: So, all were based on their personal knowledge, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Again, Sir. Based on—based on their personal knowledge?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Okay. So nothing was supplied to any agent, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I don’t understand the question. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr, Vinluan
blocks.text: So, nothing was supplied. Ibig sabihin, hindi binigay sa kahit anong agent yung isusulat niya doon sa affidavit? Independent nila ginawa lahat iyan.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: lyong affidavit ng mga wimess?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Correct, yes.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. So, in other words, what you’re telling this Court is that these are bona fide affidavits and not template affidavits, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Bona fide and not what. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Template. lyong hindi pininnahan lang basta.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, I think you can ask. Sir, the one who signed the affidavit. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Yes, so ganoon—hindi. But, again, this is part of your affi—these are attachments to your Affidavit of Investigation. So, you should have checked these at least, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, sa practice ho namin, may mga templated na talaga kung common na iyong question. Ginagawa ho talaga iyon.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Ah, okay.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Now, para mo-clarify ho kayo, pwede n 'yo hong tanungin iyong—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Thank you, thank you. So, in other words, you used template affidavits.

Let me direct your attention to the affidavit of John Mark Calilung, pages 170 to 171 of the Articles of Impeachment- G9. Allow me to read a portion of its paragraph: 5. It says there, “No joke”—can you flash that on the screen? It says there, “No joke, no joke. Nagbilin na ako”—iyan, sige. Can we focus on that part? Iyan. So here. Iyan. So, nag—”No joke.
MONDAY, JULY 13,2026
no joke. Nagbilin na ako, ma’am. ” Here. On the bottom portion. “Nagbilin na ako ma’ama, 'pag namatay ako. ”
All right. Do you confirm that there is a typographical error in the spelling of “ma’am” which was incorrectly spelled as “ma’ama”?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, Your Honor, with all due respect. Your Honor, please. What’s the relevance of a typographical error from being—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: We will demonstrate that. Your Honor. I’m just laying the basis.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: What’s the relevance? Typographical error—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: On a short leash. Counsel, show the connection and relevance.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Can we confirm first. Your Honor, if he confirms that there’s a typographical error on Calilung’s affidavit?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I think since the signatory is here—Agent Calilung and Atty. Van Angluben, sila ho ang makakasagot.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. So, he cannot confirm?

So, let me direct your attention to the affidavit of Gierran, Agent Angeli Jeaime Gierran, pages 178 to 179 of AOI’s Annex G9. Again, let me read from paragraph 5 as well. It says here—can we zoom that portion? “No joke, no joke. Nagbilin na ako, ma’ama, ‘pag namatay ako. ” So, do you confirm that there’s also a typographical error in the spelling of the word “ma’am” which was also incorrectly spelled as “ma’ama”?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: You can ask the one who signed this affidavit. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: So, you caimot confirm also?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: You can ask, Your Honor, the one who signed it.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: One last, pages 199 to 200 of Annex G9 as well. This is now the affidavit of Ria Asuncion. Can I go to paragraph 5 also? O. “No joke, no joke. Nagbilin na ako, ma’ama.” Can you also confirm that there’s a typographical error in the spelling of “ma’am” which was also incorrectly spelled as “ma’ama”?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Again, you can ask the one who signed the affidavit.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. So, in other words, all three affidavits contain the exact typographical error of the word “ma’ama,” same paragraph number, and do you still maintain that these affidavits were independently executed?

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Zubiri
blocks.text: Mr. Presiding Officer.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Before I recognize Senator-Judge Zubiri, Counsel, I think inamin naman na ni Attorney Lotoc na may mga template sila. Kumbaga, may mga de-kahon silang affidavits.
Tama ba. Attorney Lotoc?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Opo, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: So, posibleng isa ‘to sa mga dekahon n'yong affidavit? Is that possible?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor. Kasi same long ho iyong utterances, Sir. Kaya siguro iyongpag- copy-paste diyan, kung ano ho iyong unang «a-copy-paste doon sa utterance, iyon na ho iyong—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Kaya nga. So, kasiparehong number 5, parehong “ma’ama,” ‘di ba? Meaning, may point naman si Attorney—may point naman iyong Counsel for Respondent, so it’s possible na isa ‘to sa mga dekahon n’yong affidavit?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026
MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE ZUBIRI

Senator-Judge Zubiri commended the diligence of the Counsel for the Respondent in conducting the cross-examination but, considering that the proceedings had already lasted approximately six hours, he proposed that the continuation of the cross-examination be deferred until the following day.
INQUIRY OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

In response to the Presiding Officer’s inquiry. Attorney Vinluan informed the Court that the cross-examination was approximately halfway through. He also noted that it was the first opportunity for the Respondent, through Counsel, to confront the witnesses against her, explaining that she had not participated in the proceedings before the Committee on Justice of the HOR for the reasons stated in her petition for certiorari.

Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the Court was not limiting the Respondent’s right to complete the cross-examination but was merely determining whether to continue that evening or resume the proceedings the following day. Attorney Vinluan replied that the Respondent was amenable to either course but, in deference to the request of Senator-Judge Zubiri, agreed to continue the cross-examination the following day.

Thereafter, the Presiding Officer thanked Attorney Lotoc, reminding him that the subpoena requiring his appearance at the following day’s hearing remained in effect and that he would continue his testimony under the same oath previously administered, proceeding to discharge him for the day.
ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Presiding Officer Escudero informed the Court that it had received the Respondent’s memorandum on the requests for the issuance of subpoenas duces tecum for bank records, records of the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC), and the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR). Upon inquiry, the Prosecution confirmed that it had likewise submitted its memorandum.

The Presiding Officer then directed that copies of the parties’ submissions be furnished to all Senator-Judges and reminded the parties that oral arguments on the requests would be heard on Wednesday after the presentation of the Prosecution’s witnesses.
MOTION OF SENATE PRESIDENT GATCHALIAN

Upon motion of Senate President Gatchalian, following consultation with Senator-Judge Cayetano (A), the Court approved, without objection, the issuance of a subpoena requiring NBI Director Matibag to appear on Monday, July 20, 2026, at 2:00 p.m., in view of his scheduled official engagement in Bangkok the following day.
Presiding Officer Escudero emphasized that, if necessary, the witness would remain until the completion of the cross-examination, redirect, and recross-examination.
INQUIRY OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Presiding Officer Escudero inquired about the remaining witnesses to be presented by the Prosecution, and said that based on his understanding, there are two remaining witnesses, on deck, namely Atty Lopez and Captain Bello who shall be presented in that order. Representative
MONDAY, JULY 13, 2026

Defensor eonfirmed that based on their earlier manifestation, they were supposed to present Atty. Lopez following the completion of Attorney Lotoc’s testimony, including the cross-examination and, if necessary, his redirect and recross-examination. ADJOURNMENT OF THE IMPEACHMENT TRIAL The Presiding Officer declared the impeachment trial adjourned until two o’clock in the afternoon of Tuesday, July 14, 2026.
It was 8:12 p.m.
I hereby certify to the correctness of the foregoing.
TUG JR.
Approved on July 14, 2026
